---
title: "3.4 Constructors"
subtitle: "Class Creation"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit3-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Constructors initialize new objects

A <span class="term">constructor</span> (构造器) is a special block of code that runs when a new object is created.

```java
Student s = new Student("Ana", 11);
```

The constructor receives the arguments and uses them to initialize instance variables.

## Constructor Signature

### Same name as the class, no return type

```java
public class Student
{
    private String name;
    private int gradeLevel;

    public Student(String initialName, int initialGrade)
    {
        name = initialName;
        gradeLevel = initialGrade;
    }
}
```

The constructor name is `Student`, exactly matching the class name.

## Job of a Constructor

### Set a valid starting state

A constructor should put the object into a usable state.

::: {.tight-list}
- initialize each important instance variable
- use parameter values when needed
- assign default values when appropriate
- avoid leaving data uninitialized by accident
:::

## Identify Task

### Which block is the constructor?

```java
public class Fraction
{
    private int numerator;
    private int denominator;

    public Fraction(int n, int d)
    {
        numerator = n;
        denominator = d;
    }
}
```

Answer: the block beginning `public Fraction(int n, int d)`.

## Quick Check

### Constructor purpose

Which statement best describes a constructor?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. It prints an object.
- B. It initializes a new object.
- C. It always returns a `String`.
- D. It must be marked `static`.
:::

Answer: <span class="mark">B</span>. Constructors initialize new objects.

## Code Task

### Complete a `Car` constructor

```java
public class Car
{
    private String model;
    private int year;

    public Car(String carModel, int carYear)
    {
        model = carModel;
        year = carYear;
    }
}
```

Trace the call:

```java
Car c = new Car("Civic", 2024);
```

After construction, `model` is `"Civic"` and `year` is `2024`.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Student Class

Complete the class with a constructor.

```java
public class Student
{
    private String name;
    private int gradeLevel;
    private double gpa;

    public Student(String studentName, int studentGrade, double studentGpa)
    {
        name = studentName;
        gradeLevel = studentGrade;
        gpa = studentGpa;
    }
}
```

Then create two `Student` objects with different data.

## Community Class Extension

### Continue your design

Choose one class from the community design task in Topic 3.3.

Add:

::: {.tight-list}
- two private instance variables
- one constructor with parameters
- assignments from parameters to instance variables
:::

The constructor should make every new object start in a meaningful state.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- identify a constructor by name and lack of return type
- explain that constructors run when `new` creates an object
- map constructor arguments to parameter variables
- assign parameter values into instance variables
- explain why constructors should initialize all important object state
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
